rubric_json: [{"criterion": "Recognizes that the handleSend guard in src/components/ChatInput/ChatInput.tsx checks if (hasActiveModel) instead of if (!hasActiveModel), so the warning toast fires when a model IS loaded and the send proceeds when no model is active, reversing the intended safety check", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Notes that loadUserProfile in src/services/palshub/AuthService.ts compares error.code === 'PGRST116' where the original logic uses !==, causing the catch block to swallow every Supabase error except the 'no rows found' case instead of only ignoring that specific code", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Catches that getMaxPPValue in src/screens/BenchmarkScreen/BenchmarkScreen.tsx reads modelStore.activeContextSettings.n_batch instead of n_ubatch when capping the prompt-processing slider, binding the upper limit to the full batch size rather than the micro-batch size and allowing values that exceed the intended processing granularity", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Finds that getModelMemoryRequirement in src/components/MemoryRequirement/MemoryRequirement.tsx receives projectionModel as the first argument and model as the second, swapping them so the projection model's size is treated as the main model and the main model's size is treated as the projection overhead", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Spots that handlePressIn in src/components/Message/Message.tsx animates scaleAnim to 0.95 rather than 1.01, making the message container shrink on touch instead of subtly enlarging, which inverts the press-in micro-interaction feedback", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Detects that the ResponseBubble component in src/components/ResponseBubble/ResponseBubble.tsx initializes its bubbleState to BubbleState.PARTIAL instead of BubbleState.EXPANDED, causing every new response to render in the collapsed partial view with a fade mask rather than showing the full content by default", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Points out that the statusBarHeight calculation in src/components/ChatHeader/ChatHeader.tsx uses insets.top + 5 instead of insets.top - 5 for Dynamic Island devices, adding pixels to the safe area offset rather than subtracting the Dynamic Island overshoot, which pushes the header content down by 10 extra points on notched iPhones", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies that handleSaveSettings in src/components/PalGenerationSettingsSheet/PalGenerationSettingsSheet.tsx passes the raw settings state to onUpdateSettings instead of processedSettings.settings, bypassing the numeric type conversion pipeline so string values from text inputs are persisted as strings rather than numbers, causing type mismatches in downstream completion parameter consumers", "weight": 1.0}]

[{"criterion": "src/darker/chooser.py - _any_item_in_range - Identifies that the range check is inclusive on both ends (start <= n <= end) for every chunk so an edit on the last line of a multi-line chunk is counted; a bug uses exclusive end (start <= n < end) when length > 1 so the last line of multi-line chunks is excluded and that chunk gets the original instead of formatted when the only edit is on that last line. (Oscillating: only manifests for multi-line chunks.)", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "src/darker/diff.py - opcodes_to_edit_linenums - Identifies that lastline always includes the last opcode line, i.e. lastline = max(last_opcode_end + 1, last_multiline_string_end) regardless of opcode count; a bug sets lastline = last_opcode_end (no +1) when len(opcodes) == 1, so with a single opcode the last line of the edited region is excluded from yielded line numbers. (Oscillating: only when exactly one opcode is passed.)", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "src/darker/config.py - OutputMode.validate_stdout_src - Identifies that the second early-return condition is written as two disjunctive clauses: (stdin_filename is not None and len(src) == 0) or src == [\"-\"], with no parentheses around (len(src) == 0 or src == [\"-\"]). A bug groups the latter: stdin_filename is not None and (len(src) == 0 or src == [\"-\"]), which makes src == [\"-\"] valid only when stdin_filename is set; the correct code accepts src == [\"-\"] in a separate clause without requiring stdin.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "src/darker/config.py - validate_config_output_mode - Identifies that we raise when both diff and stdout are truthy (e.g. bool(config.get(...)) or value equality), and call validate_diff_stdout with those values; a bug uses identity (config.get(\"diff\") is True and config.get(\"stdout\") is True) so integer 1, string \"true\", or other truthy values from TOML/config are not treated as True and diff+stdout can both be enabled without raising. (Oscillating: only when config uses non-bool truthy values.)", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "src/darker/__main__.py - _drop_changes_on_unedited_lines - Identifies that the binary search over context lines includes the maximum valid value (full file), i.e. upper bound is max_context_lines + 1 (BinarySearch(0, max_context_lines + 1)); a bug uses max_context_lines and never tries full-file context.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "src/darker/__main__.py - main - Identifies that formatting_failures_on_modified_lines is set to True whenever a yielded file had reformatting changes (new != old), regardless of output mode; a bug sets it only when output_mode != OutputMode.CONTENT and new != old, so with -d/--stdout and --check the exit code can be 0 when changes were present.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "src/darker/concurrency.py - get_executor - Identifies that DummyExecutor is chosen by value equality (max_workers == 1), not identity (max_workers is 1); a bug uses \"is 1\" so when config or caller passes float 1.0 or another 1-valued type the condition is False and ProcessPoolExecutor is used instead of DummyExecutor, changing semantics for workers=1 in those cases.", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "src/darker/concurrency.py - DummyExecutor.submit - Identifies that the exception handler catches BaseException (not only Exception) so that KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit, and other BaseException subclasses are stored in the Future and re-raised from result(); a bug catches only Exception so those are not handled and the Future may never complete or the exception propagates from submit() instead of from result().", "weight": 1.0}]

[{"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the dewpoint() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/meteorology.py: (1) the unary negation sign has been removed from the expression * -(np.log10(_relh / 100.0)) on line 265, changing it to * (np.log10(_relh / 100.0)) which flips the sign of the entire logarithmic subexpression since log10 of a value between 0 and 1 is negative and the negation was needed to make the denominator term positive, and (2) the constant 0.000425 has been changed to 0.000524 (digits 4 and 5 transposed) in the same formula, compounding the sign error with a ~23%% magnitude error in the coefficient", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the get_summary_table() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/observation.py: (1) the logical operator between the two parenthesized conditions on line 113 was changed from 'or' to 'and', creating a logically impossible compound condition that requires valid.month == 12 AND valid.month == 1 simultaneously which can never be true since month cannot be both 12 and 1 at the same time, making the entire special-case branch dead code so the function never returns the unpartitioned 'summary' table for year-boundary dates, and (2) the boundary comparison valid.day >= 30 was changed to valid.day > 30 on line 113 which would additionally exclude December 30th even if the or/and change were not present", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the summary_update() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/observation.py: (1) the string slice col[5:] on line 187 was changed to col[4:] inside the f-string that generates SQL column names, so after the startswith('null_') check passes (where 'null_' is 5 characters: n-u-l-l-underscore), the slice index 4 instead of 5 leaves the trailing underscore in the column name producing '_tmpf' instead of 'tmpf' in the generated SQL UPDATE statement causing silent SQL errors or wrong column references, and (2) the SQL comparison operator on line 154 was changed from %(sknt)s > max_sknt to %(sknt)s >= max_sknt causing the max_sknt_ts timestamp to be unnecessarily updated on ties when the wind speed equals but does not exceed the current maximum", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the SQL operator swap in the get_grids() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/iemre.py where the modulo (%%) and integer division (/) operators in the SQL query on line 223 have been swapped: the original '(gid / %%s)::int as y, gid %%%% %%s as x' correctly decomposes a 1D grid index into row (y = gid / num_columns) and column (x = gid %% num_columns), but the bugged version '(gid %%%% %%s)::int as y, gid / %%s as x' assigns the modulo result to y and the division result to x, transposing the row and column indices so that grid data is loaded into the wrong cells of the numpy array, silently corrupting the entire spatial dataset", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the contime() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/nws/vtec.py: (1) the year threshold on line 240 was changed from ts.year < 1971 to ts.year < 1981, expanding the rejection window by a full decade so that valid VTEC timestamps from 1971 through 1980 are incorrectly treated as NWS timestamp bugs and return None instead of a valid datetime, and (2) the return statement on line 242 was changed from ts.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) to ts.replace() which removes the tzinfo keyword argument so the function returns a naive datetime object instead of a UTC-aware datetime, violating the function's docstring contract of returning a UTC datetime and causing downstream TypeError exceptions whenever the result is compared with other timezone-aware datetime objects", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the three interacting errors in the parse() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/nws/ugc.py: (1) the condition len(this_part) == 6 on line 281 was changed to len(this_part) >= 6, which subsumes the elif len(this_part) > 6 branch on line 288 making it unreachable dead code since all strings of length 6 or more are now handled by the first branch \u2014 range-expansion UGC codes like 'IAC001>005' are misprocessed as simple 6-character state codes, (2) the string slice state_code[:2] on line 286 was changed to state_code[:3] which produces a 3-character prefix instead of a 2-character state abbreviation and when combined with state_code[2] duplicates the third character creating malformed 7-character UGC codes like 'IACC001' instead of 'IAC001', and (3) range(first_val, last_val + 1) on line 305 was changed to range(first_val, last_val) dropping the last value in the sequence due to Python's exclusive upper bound, so range expansions miss their final zone/county", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the _do_sql_vtec_new() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/nws/products/_vtec_util.py: (1) the rowcount check txn.rowcount > 0 on line 512 was changed to txn.rowcount >= 0 which is an always-true tautology since Python DB-API cursor.rowcount is never negative after a successful execute (it is 0 for no rows or a positive integer for affected rows), so the duplicate-detection branch always executes regardless of whether existing records were actually found, and (2) the SQL WHERE clause status in ('NEW', 'EXB', 'EXA') on line 519 was changed to status not in ('NEW', 'EXB', 'EXA') which inverts the set of records targeted for deletion during corrections, deleting active/continued/cancelled records while preserving the new/extended records that should have been replaced", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the three interacting errors in the VTECProduct.sql() method in pyIEM/src/pyiem/nws/products/vtec.py: (1) dbdf['missed'] is initialized to False on line 75 but the loop on lines 112-119 also assigns False to matching rows, and the filter on line 122 selects rows where dbdf['missed'] is True \u2014 since every row starts False and is only ever set to False, the filter df = dbdf[dbdf['missed']] always yields an empty DataFrame so the missed-UGC detection on line 128 never triggers, (2) on line 78 the condition uses 'not check_dup_ps(segment)' which negates the duplicate-check function so the warning about duplicated VTEC phenomena/significance on line 79-82 fires for non-duplicate segments instead of actual duplicates, and (3) the ETN threshold on line 126 uses >= 100 but the comment on lines 123-125 explicitly states 'ETNs over 1000' for tropical/earthquake products, meaning the threshold is missing a zero and incorrectly suppresses missed-UGC warnings for all events with ETN >= 100 instead of only ETN >= 1000", "weight": 1.0}]

[{"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the gdd() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/meteorology.py: (1) in the ceiling-clamp operation on line 319 the third argument to np.ma.where was changed from highf to lowf breaking the parallel pattern of four symmetric clamp calls where each should reference its own variable (lines 317-320 show highf-base-highf lowf-base-lowf highf-ceiling-lowf lowf-ceiling-lowf with the third one using lowf instead of highf), and (2) the scalar detection shape comparison on line 322 was changed from res.shape == (1,) to res.shape == (1, 1) which never matches because np.ma.where returns 1-D arrays with shape (1,) not 2-D arrays with shape (1, 1) so single-element results are always returned as arrays instead of being extracted as scalar floats", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the distance.value() method in pyIEM/src/pyiem/datatypes.py: (1) the feet-to-meters input conversion constant on line 71 uses 3.82084 while the meters-to-feet output conversion on line 86 uses 3.28084 creating an inconsistency where the same physical constant has two different values within the same function (digits 2 and 8 transposed in the input path) so a round-trip conversion FT to M to FT would not return the original value, and (2) the centimeters output conversion on line 92 uses division (meters / 100.0) instead of multiplication (meters * 100.0) which inverts the scaling direction so centimeter outputs are 10000x too small since the input path for CM correctly uses meters = value / 100.0 but the output path should be the inverse operation (multiply)", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the hourly_offset() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/iemre.py: (1) the timezone guard on line 284 uses dtobj.tzinfo == timezone.utc instead of dtobj.tzinfo != timezone.utc so the condition selects timestamps that are already UTC and converts them to UTC (a no-op via astimezone) while non-UTC timestamps bypass the conversion entirely making the entire if-block dead code that never performs a meaningful timezone conversion, and (2) on line 289 the timedelta property .seconds is used instead of the method .total_seconds() and since .seconds returns only the seconds component of a timedelta (range 0-86399) discarding the days component any offset beyond the first day of the year wraps around producing hour indices in the range 0-23 instead of the correct cumulative hour index for the year", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the find_ij() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/iemre.py: (1) in the boundary validation on line 299 the first check uses lat < dom['west_edge'] instead of lon < dom['west_edge'] cross-wiring the latitude variable against a longitude boundary so points with valid longitudes but latitudes below the western edge value are incorrectly rejected or accepted depending on the domain geometry, and (2) the j-index computation on line 307 uses DX instead of DY as the grid spacing divisor so the latitude index is computed using the longitude grid spacing instead of the latitude grid spacing which produces wrong j-indices whenever DX and DY have different values", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the bounded() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/observation.py: (1) the bounds check on line 63 was changed from val < floor or val > ceiling to val <= floor or val >= ceiling changing the boundary comparison from strict to non-strict inequality so values exactly equal to the floor or ceiling are now rejected as out-of-bounds instead of being accepted, and (2) the masked array check on line 67 was changed from if np.ma.is_masked(val) to if np.ma.is_masked(val) is not None which always evaluates to True because np.ma.is_masked returns a boolean (True or False) and both True is not None and False is not None are True in Python so every ndarray input regardless of mask status is treated as masked and returns None", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the drct2text() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/util.py: (1) the compass label on line 688 was changed from 'ESE' to 'ENE' creating a duplicate ENE entry (ENE already appears on line 684 for the 57-80 degree range) so the 102-127 degree range incorrectly returns 'ENE' instead of 'ESE' and the text 'ESE' never appears in any output, and (2) the compass label on line 698 was changed from 'SW' to 'WSW' creating a duplicate WSW entry (WSW already appears on line 700 for the 237-260 degree range) so the 215-237 degree range incorrectly returns 'WSW' instead of 'SW' and the text 'SW' never appears in any output making two of the sixteen compass directions unreachable", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the compute_iemid() method of the Observation class in changed from txn.rowcount == 0 to txn.rowcount != 0 inverting the failure detection so the function returns False when the station IS found in the database and attempts to call fetchone() when no rows exist causing the subsequent row['iemid'] access to raise a TypeError on None", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the speed.value() method in pyIEM/src/pyiem/datatypes.py: (1) the KMH-to-MPS input conversion on line 148 was changed from self._value / 3.6 to self._value * 3.6 inverting the arithmetic direction so the input path and the KMH output path on line 156 (mps_value * 3.6) both multiply by 3.6 instead of being inverse operations and a round-trip KMH to MPS to KMH returns value * 3.6 * 3.6 = value * 12.96 instead of the original multiplication t * 2 so the variable t2 contains twice the temperature instead of the temperature squared making all polynomial terms that use t2 (lines 232 234 235 236 242) compute with the wrong power while the variable name t2 misleadingly suggests squaring, and (2) on line 245 np.logical_or was changed to np.logical_and creating the impossible condition (t < 80 AND t > 120) which is always np.pi to * 180.0 * np.pi so instead of dividing the arctan2 result by pi to convert from radians to degrees the code multiplies by pi producing values that are pi-squared (~9.87) times too large, and (2) the compass offset was changed from + 180 to - 180 reversing the wind convention adjustment so directions are shifted 360 degrees in the wrong direction relative to the correct meteorological convention", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the mixing_ratio() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/meteorology.py: (1) on line 295 the vapor pressure formula was changing the vapor pressure computation, and (2) on line 296 the parentheses around (1000.0 - e) were removed changing the expression from 0.62197 * e / (1000.0 - e) to 0.62197 * e / 1000.0 - e which due to Python operator precedence evaluates as ((0.62197 * e) / 1000.0) - e instead of (0.62197 * e) / (1000.0 - e) fundamentally changing the mixing ratio formula", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the dewpoint_from_pq() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/meteorology.py: (1) on line 255 the denominator was changed from (0.622 + mr) to (0.622 - mr) flipping the sign from addition to subtraction so for typical mixing ratios near 0.01 the denominator changes from ~0.632 to ~0.612 introducing a ~3%% error and for mixing ratios approaching 0.622 the denominator approaches zero causing division overflow, and (2) on line 257 the discriminant was changed from (b * b - 223.1986) to (b * b + 223.1986) flipping the sign from subtraction to addition under the to degree input producing values that are (180/pi)^2 times too large, and (2) the RAD-to-DEG conversion on line 196 was changed from self._value * 180 / pi to self._value * 180 * pi replacing division "Identifies the two interacting errors in the get_gid() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/iemre.py: (1) the grid ID formula on line 328 was changed from j * DOMAINS[domain]['nx'] + i to i * DOMAINS[domain]['ny'] + j swapping the row index j and column index i so the formula computes column-major instead of row-major linearization, and (2) the stride was changed from nx (number of {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the ddhhmm2datetime() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/util.py: (1) the day extraction slice on line 68 was changed from ddhhmm[:2] to ddhhmm[:3] taking 3 characters instead of 2 for the day field so for a typical 6-character string like '151200' the day becomes int('151') = 151 instead of int('15') = 15 which will cause a ValueError when used "Identifies the two interacting errors in the haversine_distance() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/util.py: (1) on line 827 np.cos(phi2) was changed to np.cos(phi1) so the haversine formula uses phi1 twice instead of phi1 and phi2 breaking the symmetric pattern where both endpoint latitudes should appear (the correct formula is sin^2(dphi/2) + cos(phi1)*cos(phi2)*sin^2(dlam/2) but the bugged version uses cos(phi1)*cos(phi1)), and (2) on line 828 np.minimum(a, 1.0) was changed to np.maximum(a, 1.0) so instead of clamping the intermediate value a to at most 1.0 to prevent arcsin domain errors the code clamps it to at least 1.0 making arcsin(sqrt(max(a,1.0))) always equal to arcsin(1.0) = pi/2 so all distances evaluate to pi * earth_radius = ~20015 km regardless of actual point separation", "weight": 1.0}, {"criterion": "Identifies the two interacting errors in the bearing() function in pyIEM/src/pyiem/util.py: (1) on line 853 np.arctan2 is called as arctan2(y, x) but the standard initial bearing formula requires atan2(sin(dlam)*cos(phi2), cos(phi1)*sin(phi2) - sin(phi1)*cos(phi2)*cos(dlam)) which maps to arctan2(x, y) using the function's own variable assignments on lines 851-852 so the arguments are in the wrong order swapping the sine and cosine components and rotating the result, and (2) on line 854 the modulo operand is 180 instead of 360 which directly In Python's tuple comparison, (3,) >= (3, 8) evaluates to False because the shorter tuple is considered less than the longer one when leading elements are equal. This causes all Python 3.x versions (including 3.8+) to fall through to the else branch and attempt to import the third-party importlib_metadata backport, which may not be installed.", "weight": 1.0}]
